UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores user-specific information, application settings, and authentication-related details for the MathMaster AI application. This entity manages user preferences and daily usage limits.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: User's email address, typically obtained through Google Sign-In or other authentication methods.

displayName.description: The user's display name, typically provided by the authentication system.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

lastLoginAt.description: Timestamp indicating the user's last login.

dailyProblemCount.description: Counter for the number of math problems a user has solved within the current day, used to enforce usage limits for free tiers.

lastProblemSolveDate.description: The date when the user last solved a problem, used to reset the daily problem count at the beginning of a new day.

preferredVideoQuality.description: User's preference for the quality of generated videos (e.g., 'fast' for quicker generation, 'high' for better visual fidelity).

preferredNotationStyle.description: User's preferred style for mathematical notation rendering (e.g., 'LaTeX', 'AsciiMath').

enableDailyReminders.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the user wants to receive daily practice reminders.

darkModeEnabled.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the user has enabled dark mode for the application interface.

ConversationTurn.title: ConversationTurn
ConversationTurn.description: Represents a single message or action within a conversational exchange between a user and the AI in the MathMaster AI application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ConversationTurn entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who is participating in this conversation turn. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ConversationTurn)

timestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when this conversation turn occurred.

speaker.description: Indicates who generated this message: 'user' for user input, 'ai' for AI response, or 'system' for automated system messages (e.g., 'Analyzing your problem...').

messageText.description: The text content of the message for this conversation turn, which can be a user's question, an AI's response, or a system notification.

problemImageUrl.description: Optional URL to an image if the user's message included a photo of a math problem or a screenshot. (Format: uri)

associatedMathProblemSolutionId.description: Optional reference to a MathProblemSolution entity if this conversation turn is directly related to a specific solved problem (e.g., an AI response providing a solution, or a user asking a follow-up question about an existing solution). (Relationship: MathProblemSolution 1:N ConversationTurn)

isInitialProblemQuery.description: Boolean flag indicating if this user message is the initial query for a new math problem to be solved by the AI.

MathProblemSolution.title: MathProblemSolution
MathProblemSolution.description: Represents a single math problem submitted by a user that has been successfully solved by the AI, including the original question, the AI-generated written solution, and a reference to the animated video explanation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MathProblemSolution entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this solved math problem. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N MathProblemSolution)

initialQuestionConversationTurnId.description: Reference to the ConversationTurn entity representing the user's initial question that led to the generation of this solution. (Relationship: ConversationTurn 1:1 MathProblemSolution)

questionText.description: The original mathematical problem as provided by the user, copied from the initial conversation turn for easy access and context.

questionImageUrl.description: Optional URL to an image of the problem, copied from the initial conversation turn if an image was provided. (Format: uri)

problemType.description: The identified category or domain of the math problem (e.g., 'Algebra', 'Calculus', 'Geometry', 'Trigonometry').

submissionTime.description: Timestamp when the initial problem was submitted and processing began, typically derived from the initial question's conversation turn timestamp.

solutionText.description: The complete step-by-step written solution generated by the AI, typically formatted with LaTeX for beautiful mathematical notation.

videoUrl.description: URL to the AI-generated animated video explanation, hosted in Firebase Storage, demonstrating the solution process. (Format: uri)

videoDurationSeconds.description: The duration of the generated video explanation in seconds.

isFavorite.description: Boolean flag indicating if the user has marked this specific solution as a favorite for quick access within their library.

isSaved.description: Boolean flag indicating if the user has explicitly saved this solution to their personal library for future reference.

TechnicalTerm.title: TechnicalTerm
TechnicalTerm.description: A dictionary entry for a technical term, linking it to an external educational resource like Wikipedia.

id.description: Unique identifier for the technical term.

term.description: The technical term or keyword to match in text (e.g., 'NumPy', 'TensorFlow').

url.description: The URL to the Wikipedia page or other resource.

description.description: A brief description of the term for tooltip display.

popularity.description: A score used to prioritize matches if needed.

definition.description: Stores user-specific settings, preferences, and authentication-related metadata.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: A global dictionary of technical terms and their reference URLs.

params.name: termId
params.description: The unique identifier for the technical term.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore data structure is designed for efficiency and clarity. User-specific data is nested under userProfiles for secure access. A global technicalTerms collection provides a shared dictionary for all users to reference technical concepts found in their action plans.
